verdict: Strong tuned B3, but generic GC package pushes table perception upward.
notes: Rhystic/Rift/Fierce converts games well but looks like power overlay more than commander-native package.

verdict: Best Teval default for tuned Bracket 3 pods if you want conversion without hard-lock optics.
notes: TEV-B keeps the same GC package but improves interaction/cantrip texture; source land-count issue was repaired.

verdict: Legally B3, but plays like degen control-midrange and will read hotter than TEV-A/TEV-B.
notes: Pact + Fierce + FoN plus Rhystic/Rift/Fierce package increases perceived optimization.

verdict: Most Teval-specific and one of the nastiest “technically B3” builds; social-risk monster.
notes: Field/Chasm/Crop with Strip/Waste recursion is commander-native power but frequently crosses the fun line.

verdict: Best overall Bracket-3-faithful build in the local corpus: powerful, interactive, durable, and still resource-accrual based.
notes: Package A reads like exactly what Wizards describes for Bracket 3 Upgraded.

verdict: High-pressure Muldrotha that is still B3-legal but noticeably spike-ier than MUL-A.
notes: Fierce + Pact + degen upgrades move it closer to optimized pod expectations.

verdict: On-paper B3; in practice this is Bracket-4 behavior in a 3-GC trench coat.
notes: Ancient Tomb / Mana Vault / Chrome Mox package maps directly onto Wizards’ Bracket 4 descriptors.
